task_completion.description: Plot the three known landmarks A, B, and C with correct coordinates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the GeoGebra Algebra view or Graphics view, points A=(-300, 200), B=(100, 500), and C=(400, -100) should be visible.

task_completion.description: Use the 'Circle of Position' method by constructing two loci circles.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Two circles should be visible in the Graphics view: one passing through points A and B, and another passing through points B and C.

task_completion.description: Incorporate the measured horizontal angles APB = 40° and BPC = 70° into the construction.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Algebra view should show angle objects or values of 40° and 70° (or their geometric equivalents like central angles of 80° and 140°) used to define the loci circles.

task_completion.description: Determine the coordinates of station point P at the intersection of the two circles.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A point P should be plotted at the intersection of the two loci circles. The Algebra view should show its coordinates as approximately (405.50, -99.32).

task_completion.description: Save the completed construction as 'resection_solution.ggb' in the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GeoGebra window title should display 'resection_solution.ggb', or a terminal/file manager window should show this file saved in '~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/'.

integrity.description: The agent used the GeoGebra GUI to perform the construction.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GeoGebra application open with visible construction steps, rather than just terminal commands or a text editor generating the file.

integrity.description: The agent actually performed the geometric construction rather than hardcoding the final answer.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Algebra view should list intermediate geometric objects (circles, angles, lines, intersections) rather than just a manually entered point P.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the application by writing the .ggb XML archive directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no screenshots of text editors or scripts being used to manually write the GeoGebra save file format.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Landmarks: A=(-300, 200), B=(100, 500), C=(400, -100). Angles: APB=40°, BPC=70°. Expected filename: 'resection_solution.ggb'. CRITICAL: The correct coordinates for station point P are approximately (405.50, -99.32). Do NOT look for the incorrect metadata solution of (6.64, -225.56).
